title: Build a line-following robot
description: Mount reflectance sensors and tune motor control so your robot hugs a taped course.

dialogue.text: Let's take your servo skills further. With encoders already dialed in, we'll add dual reflectance sensors so your bot can stay locked onto a taped course.

options.text: I'm in! What parts do I need?

dialogue.text: You'll need two infrared reflectance sensors, a pair of continuous-rotation servos, wheel encoder mounts, and an Arduino. Mount everything on a small chassis so both sensors sit a few millimeters above the floor.
Upload a proportional or PID loop that adjusts each wheel based on the left/right sensor readings. Calibrate the sensor thresholds on your floor, then place the bot on dark tape and watch it drive!
